confirmDeleteAsset: Are you sure you want to delete this asset?
discoveryDescription: Asset discovery performs open port scanning and service identification on target IPs for asset inventory purposes. No vulnerability detection is performed.
targetIPHint: Supports multiple IPs, comma or newline separated
assetDiscoveryTaskCreated: Asset discovery task created. View recent task status below.
createAssetDiscoveryFailed: Failed to create asset discovery task
deleteVulnConfirm: Are you sure you want to continue?
verificationReviewSuccess: Verification successful, transferred to confirmed results
inventoryResultsCollapsedDesc: There are {{count}} service identification/asset enumeration results in the current list. Hidden by default to avoid mixing with real vulnerabilities. Check "Show Inventory" to view.
candidateResultsCollapsed: Version match results collapsed by default
candidateResultsCollapsedDesc: There are {{count}} results auto-matched against the vulnerability database based on target service product names, versions, or CPE info. They default to "candidate" status and are excluded from the main vulnerability list. Confirmed results will be automatically promoted.
noNewModelDetail: No new model details for this vulnerability
noNewModelDetailDesc: This result has no occurrence/evidence data yet. The drawer continues to display legacy vulnerability fields.
candidateFindingInfo: This result is a candidate finding
candidateFindingDesc: The system identified the target service version and matched it against affected ranges in the vulnerability database. Since this is based on version inference, manual verification against actual patch status, configuration, or testing is still needed.
candidateFindingDescDerived: The system identified the target service version and matched it against affected ranges in the vulnerability database. Since this is based on version inference, manual verification is still needed. A confirmed result #{{id}} has been derived from this candidate.
verificationNotePlaceholder: Record verification basis, e.g., patch version, manual verification results, retest commands, or failure reasons.
taskNamePlaceholder: e.g., Weekly Core System Scan (leave blank for auto-generated name)
scanTypeTooltip: Only Web and Host vulnerability scan types are available for new tasks
scanTargetHint: Supports http:// or https:// URLs, one per line
ipAddressHint: Supports multiple IPs, comma or newline separated
vulnCategoryTooltip: Special scan only executes selected vulnerability categories. Templates and built-in rules are scoped accordingly.
vulnCategoryExtra: All selected by default. Simply uncheck categories you're not currently interested in.
loginUrlHint: Leave blank to auto-infer login endpoint from target URL
webScanOnlyLoginDesc: Please provide the application URL and a valid test account. Standard scan is the default; switching to Special scan restricts templates and rules to your selected categories with deeper budget.
standardScanDesc: Uses recommended general templates and rule sets. Suitable for initial scans and routine inspections.
specialScanDesc: Executes templates and built-in rules for your selected vulnerability categories with more aggressive verification budget.
usageTipDesc: Typical workflow: Select scan type -> Fill in target -> Enter credentials -> Create task. Templates and auth flows are handled automatically with default policies.
confirmDeleteTask: Are you sure you want to delete this task?
currentlyShowingDiscovery: Currently showing asset discovery tasks only
currentlyShowingVuln: Currently showing vulnerability scan tasks only
vulnCountNarrowedDesc: High/Medium/Low risk summaries in scan tasks now exclude inventory and all candidate findings. Candidate findings come from auto-matching target service versions against the vulnerability database. For environments without historical backfill, old tasks may temporarily retain the old calculation method. Detail pages show confirmed results, candidates, and inventory separately.
assetDiscoveryOnly: Asset discovery only performs port and service identification
assetDiscoveryOnlyDesc: Suitable for host inventory, open port surveying, and service version identification. No vulnerability detection is performed.

fim.title: File Integrity Monitoring
fim.description: SSH-based periodic inspection of critical directories, comparing against baseline to detect deletions, modifications, and other anomalies.
fim.confirmDeletePolicy: Are you sure you want to delete policy "{{name}}"?
fim.confirmDeletePolicyDesc: This will also remove associated target hosts, directory configs, snapshots, events, and alert records.
fim.loadPoliciesFailed: Failed to load FIM inspection data
fim.notifyChannelsPlaceholder: Select DingTalk bots configured in Alert Center
fim.notifyChannelsHint: Reuses DingTalk bots from Alert Center > Notification Channels. FIM will auto-push new alerts to selected channels.
fim.scanCycleFormat: Scan cycle: {{interval}}s | Hash mode: {{hashMode}} | Compare mode: {{compareMode}}
fim.bindHostsWarning: Please ensure SSH host keys have been added in Security > FIM > SSH Host Keys, otherwise scans will fail.
fim.targetHostRequired: Please select at least one host
fim.scanModeHintDesc: Full Hash records file size, time, and content digest. Suitable for config files and small files. Presence Only keeps file inventory and only detects deletions. Suitable for large archive directories.
fim.confirmDeleteWatchPath: Are you sure you want to delete this directory config?
fim.baselineRebuildWarning: Rebuilding baseline will treat current host state as the new normal reference. Do not rebuild if the current directory has anomalous changes.
fim.firstTimeTip: For first-time use, build a baseline first, then run manual scans to generate diff events and integrity alerts.
fim.baselineEffectiveWarning: The active baseline serves as the reference for subsequent comparisons. After rebuilding, the current host state will be treated as the new normal.
fim.baselineRebuildTriggered: Baseline rebuild triggered. Current host state will serve as the new comparison reference.
fim.loadBaselineDataFailed: Failed to load baseline management data

executions.title: Execution Records
executions.description: View baseline builds, manual scans, and scheduled scan results. Focus on whether execution happened, what was scanned, results, and failure reasons.
executions.searchHint: Search policy / host / executor / failure reason

retryValidation.policyNotExist: Policy no longer exists, cannot retry
retryValidation.policyDisabled: Policy is disabled, please enable it first
retryValidation.hostNotInScope: Host is no longer in policy scope, cannot retry
retryValidation.hostDisabled: Host binding is disabled, please enable it first
retryValidation.noWatchPaths: Policy has no monitoring directories configured, cannot retry

error.executionAlreadyRunning: Policy already has an active execution on this host, please try again later
error.targetNotFound: Host is not in the policy scope or binding has expired
error.noWatchPathsConfigured: Policy has no watch paths configured, please configure directories first
error.recordNotFound: Related policy or host record not found, please refresh and try again
error.invalidRequest: Invalid request parameters, please check and try again
error.invalidPolicyOrSnapshot: Invalid object reference, please refresh the page and try again
error.sshExecutionFailed: SSH execution failed, please check host connectivity and FIM SSH configuration
error.scanFailed: Inspection failed, please try again later
error.buildBaselineFailed: Baseline build failed, please try again later

fimEvents.title: File Change Events
fimEvents.description: Shows diff results between baseline and current inspection snapshots, making it easy to identify what changed.
fimEvents.confirmDeleteEvent: Are you sure you want to delete this event?
fimEvents.confirmDeleteEventDesc: Deleting the event will also delete its associated alerts.
fimEvents.loadEventsFailed: Failed to load file change events

fimAlerts.title: Integrity Alerts
fimAlerts.description: Focus on integrity anomalies requiring attention. Supports host and policy dimension views with basic status flow.
fimAlerts.confirmDeleteAlert: Are you sure you want to delete this alert?

fimKnownHosts.title: FIM Host Key Whitelist
fimKnownHosts.strictModeNotice: Strict mode: Only whitelisted host keys can connect. Keys must be configured in System Settings > FIM SSH.
fimKnownHosts.addFIMTargetNotice: Add SSH host public keys here before adding FIM target servers. This prevents man-in-the-middle attacks.
fimKnownHosts.getPublicKeyMethod: How to get the host public key:
fimKnownHosts.getPublicKeyStep1: On the target server, run: cat /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key.pub
fimKnownHosts.getPublicKeyStep2: Or use the 'Import' feature on this page to import from known_hosts file
fimKnownHosts.publicKeyStep1: On the target server run: cat /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key.pub
fimKnownHosts.publicKeyStep2: Or use the 'Import' feature on this page to import from known_hosts
fimKnownHosts.publicKeyStep3: Paste the complete public key content (including key type prefix)
fimKnownHosts.hostDescription: Host description or note, e.g., Production Server - App01
fimKnownHosts.confirmDeleteHost: Are you sure you want to delete this host key?
fimKnownHosts.confirmDeleteHostDesc: After deletion, FIM scans will not be able to connect to this host. Please ensure this server is no longer monitored.
fimKnownHosts.confirmDeleteHostShort: Are you sure you want to delete this host key?
fimKnownHosts.confirmDeleteHostShortDesc: After deletion, FIM scans will not be able to connect to this host.
fimKnownHosts.importFromKnownHostsDesc: Paste or upload known_hosts format content. Duplicate keys will be automatically skipped.
fimKnownHosts.importFormatExampleDesc:
Format 1: Default port
server.example.com ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI...

Format 2: Custom port
[server.example.com]:2222 ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI...
fimKnownHosts.dragUploadHint: Click or drag to upload CSV data package
fimKnownHosts.uploadThenPaste: After upload, content is automatically read. You can also paste directly in the text box below.

vulnDB.title: Vulnerability Knowledge Base
vulnDB.subtitle: Local vulnerability data browsing and search
vulnDB.externalSyncDesc: Online sync is disabled on the platform. Please complete NVD/CNVD/CNNVD data preparation externally, then import CSV packages via this page.
vulnDB.searchCVEIdTitle: Search CVE ID, title, product name
vulnDB.importInstructionDesc: Complete vulnerability data sync and cleansing externally first, then upload the generated CSV file here. The platform no longer initiates online sync.
vulnDB.clickOrDragUpload: Click or drag to upload CSV data package
vulnDB.uploadHint: After upload, content is automatically read. You can also paste directly in the text box below.
vulnDB.pasteCSVContent: Paste CSV content here, or upload a local CSV file first.
vulnDB.pleaseUploadData: Please upload or paste vulnerability data first

taskNameExample: e.g., Office network asset survey (leave blank for auto-generated name)
assistantQuickActionsDesc: Use the AI assistant sidebar to query based on the current vulnerability list context
emptyWebTargetError: Please enter a valid web target URL first
scanTaskAssistantQuickActionsDesc: Use the AI assistant sidebar to query based on the current scan task list context
pleaseEnterAtLeastOneIP: Please enter at least one target IP

webScanPrecheck.accessLabel: Does this site require login to access
webScanPrecheck.authExtra: If login requires signing, encrypted passwords, tenant parameters, or dynamic tokens, select "Custom Auth".
webScanPrecheck.sessionExtra: The clearer the session reuse, the more stable the auto-scan.
webScanPrecheck.assistantHint: If you're unsure about login method, session reuse, or whether custom auth is needed, let the Ops Assistant provide an additional layer of judgment.

title.direct: This site appears to be a standard login site
title.custom: This site may need additional authentication adaptation
title.blocked: Auto-scan is currently not recommended for this site

desc.direct: It is recommended to first create a standard post-login web scan task. If the auto-generated auth_flow is unstable, switch to custom authentication.
desc.custom: It is recommended to let the Ops Assistant evaluate the login flow first, then decide whether to generate or adjust the auth_flow.
desc.blocked: It is recommended to manually review the login flow first to confirm whether there are CAPTCHAs, SMS, QR codes, or device verification, before deciding on automated scanning.

reason.noTarget: Target URL not yet provided; unable to determine if direct scanning is suitable.
reason.publicAccess: This site appears to allow anonymous access. The current web vulnerability scanning pipeline is more suited for post-login scanning.
reason.customAuth: The login flow involves signatures, encrypted passwords, tenant identifiers, or dynamic parameters, typically requiring a custom auth_flow.
reason.captcha: The login flow depends on CAPTCHAs, SMS, QR codes, or bot detection, making it currently unsuitable for automated scanning.
reason.mixedSession: Post-login sessions depend on both Cookie and Header mixed states. It is recommended to manually confirm the session reuse method first.
reason.unknownSession: Session carrier is not yet clear. It is recommended to first confirm whether post-login uses Cookie or Header Token.
reason.noAccount: No test account provided. Even if a task is created, login state verification will be difficult.
reason.openEndpoint: The target URL or login endpoint appears to have open API or token-based login characteristics. It is recommended to perform an access pre-check first.
reason.standard: Based on current information, this appears to be a standard username/password + reusable session site. You can proceed with the standard workflow.

assistant.query1: Please help me determine whether this website can be directly integrated for post-login web vulnerability scanning.
assistant.query2: If direct integration is not possible, please only answer: what key information is needed, or why automated scanning is temporarily not recommended.

targetUrlHintDesc: Post-login web scan only supports URL lists; one URL per line, must include http:// or https://
targetIPHintDesc: One IP or IP range per line (e.g., 192.168.1.1-10)
hostVulnScanDesc: Detects security vulnerabilities in SSH, databases, Redis, and other services
webVulnScanDesc: Uses Nuclei to detect web application vulnerabilities
webScanSuggestionDesc: Prefer entering application URLs directly. By default, it will auto-login and discover pages/APIs first, then run Nuclei template probes on discovered endpoints.
standardScanCardDesc: Suitable for initial scans. Uses recommended general web templates and rule sets with broad coverage.
specialScanCardDesc: Performs more aggressive targeted verification on selected categories with deeper budget by default. Ideal for re-testing and deep scanning of key targets.
specialWebScanAlertDesc: Executes templates and built-in rules for your selected vulnerability categories with more aggressive verification budget. Suitable for targeted re-testing and deep scanning of key targets.
standardWebScanAlertDesc: Uses recommended general web templates and rule sets. Suitable for initial scans and routine inspections.
autoDiscoveryScanDesc: This creation entry defaults to auto-discovery strategy. Best for initial scanning of SPAs, post-login systems, and real business APIs.
vulnCategoryExtraSpecial: All selected by default. Special scan will only execute the categories you keep with deeper verification budget.
webScanAuthDesc: Web scanning has been adjusted to post-login only. Please provide a test account, password, and optional login endpoint. The authentication flow will be generated automatically.
loginSessionHandlingDesc: The system will auto-generate the authentication flow based on the current target. Anonymous scanning is disabled.
recommendedStep1: 1. First fill in the application URL and a valid test account
recommendedStep2: 2. Use Standard Scan for initial scans. Switch to Special Scan for re-testing or deep scanning of key targets
recommendedStep3: 3. Special Scan selects all categories by default; remove any you don't need
recommendedStep4: 4. Login URL is optional; the system will auto-infer if left blank
hostVulnScanConfigDesc: Host vulnerability scan will automatically detect security vulnerabilities in SSH, databases, Redis, and other services. No additional configuration required.
selectScanTypeFirst: Please select a web or host vulnerability scan type to continue configuration.
hostVulnDetectionDesc: Will detect host-level vulnerabilities such as SSH weak passwords, unauthorized database access, unauthorized Redis access, etc.
webAndHostVulnDetectionDesc: Will detect web vulnerabilities and host-level vulnerabilities (SSH, databases, Redis, etc.)
scanWillStartImmediately: Scan will start immediately after creation
scanMayTakeLongTime: The scan may take a while to complete. Please be patient. You can check progress in the task list.
taskCreatedDesc: The scan task has been submitted and is running in the background. You can view progress in the task list.
enterTargetUrlFirst: Please enter the target URL first
discoverySummaryDesc: Asset discovery only performs open port detection and service identification for asset inventory purposes. It does not include vulnerability detection. You can select key hosts from the results and then initiate a host or web vulnerability scan.
scanModeExplanationDesc: The current detail view prioritizes the runtime summary. It distinguishes input entries, discovered targets, actually scanned targets, rule-only targets, and targets skipped by budget for easier coverage assessment.
partialTargetsOnlyRule: Some targets only ran rule detection
partialTargetsOnlyRuleDesc: This task has {{ruleOnly}} low-value target(s) that did not run full Nuclei and only performed built-in rule detection. This is standard scan budget control and does not indicate scan failure.
targetsSkippedByBudget: Some discovered targets were skipped by budget
targetsSkippedByBudgetDesc: This task has {{skipped}} low-priority target(s) that did not enter the verification phase. If these targets are critical endpoints, consider using a Special Scan for re-testing.
browserFallbackOccurredDesc: {{fallback}} entry/entries fell back from browser discovery to HTTP discovery due to browser helper being unreachable or failing. The scan pipeline remains functional, but coverage is typically narrower than browser-mode.
taskStopNoteDesc: The current task will stop after the current step completes. To continue execution, please create a new task.
resultInterpretationDesc: Only confirmed results are included in risk scoring and the main report conclusions. Pending verification results are for investigation only. Asset info is for inventory and subsequent scan routing, and does not count toward risk.
confirmedGuidance: Included in risk statistics, handling, and exported main conclusions
candidateGuidance: Not included in formal risk by default; verification against version, configuration, and manual review is recommended
inventoryGuidance: Used for asset inventory and subsequent scan routing
versionMatchGroupedCandidate: Version match results grouped under Pending Verification
versionMatchGroupedCandidateDesc: {{count}} result(s) from this scan were auto-matched against the vulnerability database based on target service product names, versions, or CPE information. They are excluded from risk scoring and confirmed result statistics by default; confirmed results will be additionally created upon successful verification.
inventorySeparatedDesc: This scan also found {{count}} service identification/version enumeration result(s). These are preserved for asset inventory but excluded from risk scoring and real vulnerability statistics.
discoveryResultsSummary: Identified {{assetCount}} port and service record(s) across {{hostCount}} host(s).
noOpenPortsDesc: No open ports or service responses were detected for the current targets. Check whether the target IPs are reachable, the network range is correct, or retry later.
noTemplateMatchDesc: This typically means the current target URL did not match any existing templates and does not imply the target is absolutely safe. Consider scanning real business APIs, post-login endpoints, or switching to Special Scan.
resultsNeedVerificationDesc: These results were auto-matched against the vulnerability database based on target service product names, versions, or CPE information. They serve as investigation leads and should not be treated as confirmed vulnerabilities directly.
resultsForInventory: The following results are for asset inventory
resultsForInventoryDesc: These results typically come from service identification, version enumeration, or information probing. They do not represent confirmed exploitable vulnerabilities.
scanEntriesSummary: Showing {{count}} scan entr(y/ies). Results prioritize the new targets model, displaying parent-child relationships, entry types, sources, and resolved host and service information.
newTargetModel: This tab now uses the new target model
newTargetModelDesc: This tab prioritizes security_scan_targets over legacy discoveries. The parent-child hierarchy more closely reflects the actual discovery and verification chain.
noTargetModelData: No new target model data available for this task
noTargetModelDataDesc: This tab only reads security_scan_targets. If empty, it means this task has not yet completed new model persistence, or the historical data has not been migrated.
entryTableNotFullNuclei: The entry table does not mean all entries ran full Nuclei
entryTableNotFullNucleiDesc: Among the displayed entries, {{ruleOnly}} target(s) only ran rule detection, and {{skipped}} target(s) were skipped by budget. Consult the summary card to assess actual coverage.
discoveryChainBrowserFallback: Browser fallback detected in the discovery chain
discoveryChainBrowserFallbackDesc1: This task had at least one browser discovery fallback to HTTP discovery. Most recent reason: {{reason}}
discoveryChainBrowserFallbackDesc2: This task had at least one browser discovery fallback to HTTP discovery. Results may be more conservative.
webFollowupMultiHint: Multiple entries were auto-discovered and scanned, but no template rules were matched. Consider continuing with a Special Scan on the current entries, or re-testing authentication, parameters, and permission differences on key endpoints.
webFollowupSingleHint: No template rules were matched in this scan. If the current target is a single entry, consider switching to auto-discovery scan, or supplementing with more specific business URLs / API URLs for re-testing.
associatedCandidateNote: There are currently {{count}} clue(s) auto-matched against the vulnerability database based on target service product names, versions, or CPE information. They have been moved to the "Pending Verification" tab.
